title: Context-aware prompt mutation in self-evolve pipeline
description: Replace any fixed/templated mutation strategies in the self-evolve pipeline with an LLM agent call that reads the current prompt + recent task outcomes and generates context-aware mutations. Currently the 'Prompt Self-Mutation' skill likely applies predetermined variation patterns (e.g. paraphrase, add constraint, reorder steps). AVO's insight is that agent-as-operator makes the mutation itself context-sensitive — it can reason about *why* a prompt underperformed before mutating it, not just how to vary its surface form.
rationale: The note identifies that fixed evolutionary operators encode a prior assumption: 'you already know what a good mutation looks like.' Mira's self-evolve task faces the same problem — templated mutations (synonym swap, constraint injection, restructuring) are context-blind. The AVO inversion says: give the mutation step access to the task trace (what failed, what the prompt was, what the output was) and let an agent call decide *what kind* of mutation is warranted. This is structurally identical to what the note calls 'context-aware operators.' The note also flags overhead risk — agent mutation is more expensive than a template — which maps directly to the `CLAUDE_TIMEOUT_THINK: 120s` budget. The fix should gate agent-mutation on prompts that have failed ≥N times, not run it on every cycle.
diff_description: In the self-evolve handler: (1) Add a failure_count field to each tracked prompt/skill entry in soul state. (2) Change the mutation step: if failure_count < threshold (e.g. 2), use the existing lightweight template mutation; if failure_count >= threshold, invoke an LLM agent call with the full task trace (prompt text + last 3 outputs + failure reason) and ask it to propose a targeted mutation with explicit reasoning. (3) Log the mutation rationale alongside the new prompt version so future reflect cycles can evaluate whether context-aware mutations outperform blind ones. Threshold and the agent call prompt should be config values in soul_manager or a new self_evolve_config block.
